title: Fix: PurgeMissingSessions wipes DB on empty scan result
description:
Critical bug: if ScanSessions returns an empty slice (e.g. ~/.claude temporarily inaccessible), PurgeMissingSessions deletes ALL sessions and cascade-deletes all tags. Total data loss from a transient filesystem issue.

Also related: WalkDir callback returns nil on errors (scanner.go:39), silently skipping files. Those skipped sessions appear missing and get purged.

Both issues feed into the same purge-safety problem.
requirements: In internal/db/queries.go PurgeMissingSessions: if existingIDs is empty, return early without deleting (do NOT delete all sessions). Add a comment explaining why.

In internal/indexer/scanner.go WalkDir callback: when err != nil, log the error and return nil (continue walking), but also set a flag or collect errors. If any walk errors occurred, do NOT purge — only purge when the scan completed cleanly.
In internal/indexer/indexer.go run(): accept a 'scanHadErrors' signal from ScanSessions. If true, skip PurgeMissingSessions and log a warning that purge was skipped due to scan errors.
Update ScanSessions signature to return ([]SessionFile, bool, error) where the bool indicates whether any walk errors were encountered (partial scan).
Add tests: (1) PurgeMissingSessions with empty slice does NOT delete sessions, (2) ScanSessions with inaccessible subdirectory returns partial=true and still returns accessible files
Verify the project compiles with 'go build ./cmd/ccs/' and all tests pass with 'go test ./...'
